attributes.magic: PE32 executable (console) Intel 80386, for MS Windows

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects WannaCry ransomware activity
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Tom U. @c_APT_ure (collection), oscd.community, Jonhnathan Ribeiro

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Delete shadow copy via WMIC
Detects the use of the bcdedit command to tamper with the boot configuration data. This technique is often times used by malware or attackers as a destructive way before launching ransomware.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Usage of Renamed Sysinternals Tools - RegistrySet
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects non-sysinternals tools setting the "accepteula" key which normally is set on sysinternals tool execution

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Process Explorer Driver Creation By Non-Sysinternals Binary
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects creation of the Process Explorer drivers by processes other than Process Explorer (procexp) itself.
Hack tools or malware may use the Process Explorer driver to elevate privileges, drops it to disk for a few moments, runs a service using that driver and removes it afterwards.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Shadow Copies Deletion Using Operating Systems Utilities
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Shadow Copies deletion using operating systems utilities
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Michael Haag, Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)

values.Description: Command Line Interface for Microsoft® Volume Shadow Copy Service
Command Line Interface for Microsoft® Volume Shadow Copy Service

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects loading of known vulnerable drivers via their hash.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Schedule Task Creation From Env Variable Or Potentially Suspicious Path Via Schtasks.EXE
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects Schtask creations that point to a suspicious folder or an environment variable often used by malware

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Change PowerShell Policies to an Insecure Level
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects changing the PowerShell script execution policy to a potentially insecure level using the "-ExecutionPolicy" flag.
Detects suspicious PowerShell download command

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects suspicious PowerShell download command

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious DNS Query for IP Lookup Service APIs
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects DNS queries for IP lookup services such as "api.ipify.org" originating from a non browser process.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Usage Of Web Request Commands And Cmdlets - ScriptBlock
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the use of various web request commands with commandline tools and Windows PowerShell cmdlets (including aliases) via PowerShell scriptblock logs
Detects suspicious PowerShell download command

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects installation of NalDrv or PROCEXP152 services via registry-keys to non-system32 folders.
Both services are used in the tool Ghost-In-The-Logs (https://github.com/bats3c/Ghost-In-The-Logs), which uses KDU (https://github.com/hfiref0x/KDU)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Potentially Suspicious Desktop Background Change Using Reg.EXE
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects the execution of "reg.exe" to alter registry keys that would replace the user's desktop background.
This is a common technique used by malware to change the desktop background to a ransom note or other image.

values.ParentCommandLine: "C:\Windows\system32\cmd.exe" /C reg add "HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop" /v Wallpaper /t REG_SZ /d "C:\Users\george\AppData\Roaming\hesM1y5a.bmp" /f & reg add "HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop" /v WallpaperStyle /t REG_SZ /d "0" /f & reg add "HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop" /v TileWallpaper /t REG_SZ /d "0" /f

"C:\Windows\system32\cmd.exe" /C reg add "HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop" /v Wallpaper /t REG_SZ /d "C:\Users\george\AppData\Roaming\hesM1y5a.bmp" /f & reg add "HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop" /v WallpaperStyle /t REG_SZ /d "0" /f & reg add "HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop" /v TileWallpaper /t REG_SZ /d "0" /f

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects suspicious ways to download files or content using PowerShell

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Copy From or To System Directory
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects a suspicious copy operation that tries to copy a program from system (System32, SysWOW64, WinSxS) directories to another on disk.
Often used to move LOLBINs such as 'certutil' or 'desktopimgdownldr' to a different location with a different name in order to bypass detections based on locations.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Markus Neis, Tim Shelton (HAWK.IO), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Usage Of Web Request Commands And Cmdlets
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the use of various web request commands with commandline tools and Windows PowerShell cmdlets (including aliases) via CommandLine
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: James Pemberton / @4A616D6573, Endgame, JHasenbusch, oscd.community, Austin Songer @austinsonger

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects non-interactive PowerShell activity by looking at the "powershell" process with a non-user GUI process such as "explorer.exe" as a parent.
Detects the creation of scheduled tasks by user accounts via the "schtasks" utility.
Detects command lines that contain the 'accepteula' flag which could be a sign of execution of one of the Sysinternals tools

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_category: Malware Command and Control Activity Detected
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: ET MALWARE MSIL/Matrix Ransomware CnC Activity
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"ET MALWARE MSIL/Matrix Ransomware CnC Activity"; flow:established,to_server; http.method; content:"GET"; http.uri; content:".php?apikey="; content:"&compuser="; distance:0; content:"&sid="; distance:0; content:"&phase="; distance:0; http.user_agent; content:"Mozilla/4.0 (compatible|3b 20|Synapse)"; fast_pattern; bsize:33; http.header_names; content:!"Referer|0d 0a|"; reference:md5,ad8a7a383971ce0f5fc51e909e406996; classtype:command-and-control; sid:2024120; rev:3; metadata:affected_product Windows_XP_Vista_7_8_10_Server_32_64_Bit, attack_target Client_Endpoint, created_at 2017_02_24, deployment Perimeter, former_category MALWARE, malware_family Ransomware, malware_family Matrix, signature_severity Major, tag Ransomware, updated_at 2020_08_04, mitre_tactic_id TA0040, mitre_tactic_name Impact, mitre_technique_id T1486, mitre_technique_name Data_Encrypted_for_Impact;)

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: INDICATOR-COMPROMISE Request for external IP address detected
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"INDICATOR-COMPROMISE Request for external IP address detected"; flow:to_server,established; http_header; content:"Host: myexternalip.com",fast_pattern,nocase; http_uri; content:"/raw"; http_header; content:!"User-Agent|3A|"; metadata:policy max-detect-ips drop; service:http; reference:url,virustotal.com/en/file/9a8d73cb7069832b9523c55224ae4153ea529ecc50392fef59da5b5d1db1c740/analysis/; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:42082; rev:2; )

alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"MALWARE-CNC Win.Ransomware.Matrix variant outbound connection"; flow:to_server,established; http_uri; content:"/addrecord.php?apikey=",fast_pattern,nocase; content:"compuser="; content:"sid="; content:"phase="; metadata:impact_flag red,policy balanced-ips drop,policy max-detect-ips drop,policy security-ips drop; service:http; reference:url,www.virustotal.com/gui/file/9e1e66c578d98f45a3d45cb9b9587dd1c01b98f4f75c705cab40a3c94500e992/detection; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:50634; rev:1; )

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: NETBIOS SMB srvsvc named pipe creation attempt
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert tcp any any -> $HOME_NET 445 ( msg:"NETBIOS SMB srvsvc named pipe creation attempt"; flow:to_server,established; content:"|FE|SMB|40 00|",depth 6,offset 4; content:"|05 00|",within 2,distance 6; content:"s|00|r|00|v|00|s|00|v|00|c|00|",fast_pattern,nocase; metadata:policy max-detect-ips drop; service:netbios-ssn; classtype:misc-activity; sid:38320; rev:2; )
